sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund Phase 2 of a production Cardano-native ticketing platform operated by Sellout.io and built by Anvil Development Agency.
Recipient: Anvil Development Agency, Inc. with Intersect acting as budget administrator by prior agreement

Deliverables: Secondary marketplace with on-chain settlement, Smart-contract-level royalty enforcement, Per-event anti-scalping controls, Custodial wallet onboarding inside Sellout's existing flow, Organizer tools and dashboard, Independent third-party security audit with report and remediation, Public launch campaign and trade-show activations, Public metrics dashboard and quarterly reporting, Revenue-share repayment mechanism to the Cardano Treasury

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Sellout has invested over $130,000 of its own capital into Phase 1.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, over 8 months and inclusive of a 3% Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Phase 2 is intended to deliver a secondary marketplace, royalty enforcement, anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding, organizer tools, a third-party security audit, and launch amplification.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The team commits to repay $1,093,231 to the Cardano Treasury through a revenue-share mechanism using 25% of all fees until full reimbursement and 5% thereafter.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The exact revenue-base definition and repayment cadence are not finalized in the proposal and are deferred to M1 contracting with the appointed oversight committee.
missing: Independent evidence for: Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification.
Independent evidence for: Sellout has invested over $130,000 of its own capital into Phase 1.

Independent evidence for: The proposal projects 12-month launch targets of 200+ events on-chain, 75,000+ CIP-68 NFT tickets, 150,000+ on-chain interactions, $6M+ ticket revenue on-chain, and 20,000+ new wallets.

Independent evidence for: Anvil claims a track record of 250+ Cardano projects delivered, 456,000+ assets minted, 50,000,000+ ADA staked through Anvil infrastructure, and 267,000+ on-chain transactions.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, approximately $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, over 8 months and inclusive of a 3% Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the ticketing platform is already live on Cardano mainnet and includes on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
